title: Route catalog extraction
summary: Extract authored route definitions toward a clearer catalog boundary so future Route v2 and Source to Shore work can add content without crowding controller/runtime files.

execution_order.goal: Find the safest route-data boundary and propose a behavior-preserving extraction plan.
execution_order.why_now: Source to Shore has proven the route system works, so route authoring should get easier before another content wave.

execution_order.goal: Extract route definitions into a clearer catalog module or equivalent existing-pattern boundary without changing gameplay behavior.
execution_order.why_now: Cleaner route data reduces future risk when adding or tuning beats.

execution_order.goal: Review the extraction for behavior parity, import clarity, test coverage, and authoring readability.
execution_order.why_now: Data extraction should make the repo easier to build on, not just move complexity around.

completion_gate: Authored route data has a clearer boundary than the current crowded runtime/controller surface.
Existing route ids, evidence slots, ordered evidence rules, support behavior, and filed states stay behaviorally identical.
Any authoring documentation touched by the extraction reflects the new boundary.
guardrails: Do not rename route ids or evidence ids unless scout identifies a failing typo with tests.
Do not add new route beats, route types, planner UI, save fields, or support mechanics.
Prefer existing module patterns over a broad new architecture.
Keep Source to Shore semantics stable while moving data.

scout_contract.implementation_dependency: Keep ECO-20260428-main-452 parked until ECO-20260428-critic-451 approves packet 170 route-flow consolidation.
scout_contract.pressure_point: src/engine/field-requests.ts is 1646 lines and mixes FIELD_REQUEST_DEFINITIONS, definition shape types, route unlock logic, active request resolution, evidence-slot progression, support-facing helpers, and filed-note synthesis.

preferred_boundary.compatibility: Import the catalog into src/engine/field-requests.ts and re-export FIELD_REQUEST_DEFINITIONS plus the public FieldRequestDefinition type from field-requests.ts so existing callers and tests do not churn.

main_452_scope: Move the full request-definition array together, including compatibility requests, surveys, Route v2 outings, High Pass, and Source to Shore, because unlock order currently depends on one ordered catalog.
Keep the catalog under src/engine for now because the definitions encode gameplay-route semantics rather than pure biome content.
Do not replace current .find(...) lookups with a map or generated index in this pass; preserve first-match behavior.
Update docs/architecture.md and docs/content-authoring.md only to point authors to the new catalog boundary.
Do not move Source to Shore display-state logic; packet 170 owns that behavior consolidation.
parity_tests: npm test -- --run src/test/field-season-board.test.ts src/test/save-snapshots.test.ts -t "Source to Shore|High Pass|route-marker|debug save snapshots|copy budget"
out_of_scope: No route ids, evidence ids, slot-order, unlock-chain, title, note-text, process-focus, world-state-focus, or completion-trigger changes.
No new route constants, generated indexes, route types, planner UI, save fields, or support mechanics.
No source-to-shore-state.ts behavior move until packet 170 has clean critic signoff.

post_packet_path: Use the clearer route catalog as the baseline for future beta route authoring.
If extraction exposes route-shape debt, add follow-up packets instead of widening this one.
